description: List of electricity-only smart meter plans available in the local electricity market. A plan is either conventional (repeating 'Rates' describe every day) or dynamic (a 'Dynamic' block carries the supplier's wholesale-tracking terms; the app downloads the market year's half-hourly prices and generates 365 single-day rates locally). A dynamic plan with no 'Rates' is valid — it imports as 'pending' and materialises in the background. Dynamic plans always EXPORT terms-only: the generated prices are derived from market data that must not be redistributed, and they regenerate locally on import.

Active.description: Indicates whether the plan is currently available for sign-up. Defaults to true when absent.

Bonus cash.description: Cash reward provided by the supplier for signing up (typically €0 if not offered). Defaults to 0 when absent.

DeemedExport.description: Whether the plan includes deemed export payments for surplus electricity (typically false for electricity-only plans). Always false on generated dynamic plans — their export is priced per half-hour or by 'Feed'.

Feed.description: Supplier feed-in tariff rate in cents per kWh for exporting excess electricity to the grid. Used as the flat fallback when the plan carries no SELL rates (see 'RateType'); a dynamic plan whose terms include 'FeedMultiplier'/'FeedAdder' generates time-varying SELL rates that take precedence. Defaults to 0 when absent.

Dynamic.description: Supplier terms of a dynamic (wholesale-tracking) tariff. Present ⇒ the plan's import prices are derived per half-hour as clamp(wholesale × Multiplier + Adder, Floor, Cap) from a 12-month backtest window, and 'Rates' becomes optional: terms + no rates = a pending plan whose prices the app fetches and materialises in the background. Editing materialised day rates directly is not supported — change the terms or window and regenerate.

Market.description: Identifier of the wholesale market / price feed the tariff tracks. 'ISEM-DAM' = I-SEM Day-Ahead via SEMOpx (Ireland). 'GB-AGILE-<letter>' = Octopus Agile for one GB grid-supply-point region A–P; Agile publishes final retail prices, so its terms are Multiplier 1 / Adder 0.

Year.description: First calendar year of the 12-month backtest window (a backtest: yearly costs are computed as if that window repeated). With PeriodStartMonth = 7 the window is July of this year through June of the next. Optional in a shared/imported file — when absent the app defaults to the most recent complete 12 months. Set on the plan once materialised.

PeriodStartMonth.description: First month (1-12) of the 12-month backtest window. Absent or 1 = a legacy calendar-year plan (January–December of Year). A rolling window (e.g. 7) sits inside the market's freshest ~12-month publication window, avoiding the perpetual calendar-year data gap (SEMOpx/Ireland).

Multiplier.description: Factor applied to the wholesale price (c/kWh) before the adder. Required for the terms to be complete.

Adder.description: Fixed amount in cents per kWh added after the multiplier (the supplier's margin/levies). Required for the terms to be complete.

Cap.description: Optional ceiling in cents per kWh on the derived import price.

Floor.description: Optional floor in cents per kWh on the derived import price (e.g. 0 to shield the customer from negative wholesale prices).

FeedMultiplier.description: Optional export transform: factor on the wholesale price. When this or 'FeedAdder' is present the plan gains time-varying SELL rates (unclamped — Cap/Floor bound imports only) and the scalar 'Feed' becomes unused.

FeedAdder.description: Optional export transform: cents per kWh added to wholesale × FeedMultiplier.

SourceRef.description: Provenance of the fetched price series (dataset names, fetch date, licence note). Written by the app on materialisation; informational on import.

LastUpdate.description: The last date when this plan was updated in the system.

Location.description: Optional ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code the supplier operates in (e.g. 'IE' for Ireland, 'GB' for the United Kingdom). Plans tagged with a country other than the device's are hidden and auto-deactivated. Omit or leave empty ('') for plans available everywhere.

Plan.description: The official name of the electricity plan as defined by the supplier.

Rates.description: A collection of rate structures defining different electricity costs based on days and times. Required for conventional plans; optional (and omitted on export) when a 'Dynamic' block is present — the app regenerates a dynamic plan's 365 single-day rates from the terms and the fetched market year.

Days.description: Days of the week the rate applies. 0 = Sunday, 6 = Saturday.

startDate.description: The start date when this rate is applicable.

endDate.description: The end date after which this rate is no longer valid. Generated dynamic plans use 365 single-day rates (startDate == endDate).

RateType.description: Marks an EXPORT (feed-in) rate set: 'sell' rates price exported energy per time slot, overriding the scalar 'Feed'. Absent = an import (buy) rate — pre-existing files are unchanged. Buy and sell rate sets are validated independently; each must tile the full year/day.

MinuteRange.description: A breakdown of energy pricing throughout a 24-hour period using minute-based intervals.

cost.description: Electricity unit rate in cents per kWh for the specified time range.

startMinute.description: The minute of the day when the rate starts (0 = midnight, 1440 = end of day).

endMinute.description: The minute of the day when the rate ends (e.g., 480 = 08:00 AM).

Reference.description: A direct URL to the supplier’s official plan details or tariff page. On generated dynamic plans the app overwrites this with the pricing formula and the price series' provenance.

Restrictions.description: Limitations or conditions associated with the plan. Not applicable to dynamic plans (restrictions key on repeating rate values, which a dynamic plan does not have).

active.description: Indicates whether any specific restrictions apply to this plan.

restrictionEntries.description: An array of restriction entries, such as usage limits or excess charges.

excessCost.description: The cost per kWh exceeding a defined usage limit.

limit.description: Maximum allowable usage before excess charges apply.

scope.description: The applicable rate affected by this restriction (e.g., must match a 'cost' value from MinuteRange in Rates).

Standing charges.description: Fixed daily or monthly charge applied regardless of usage, typically covering grid maintenance. Defaults to 0 when absent.

Supplier.description: The electricity provider offering this plan (e.g., Bord Gáis Energy, SSE Airtricity).
